name: J. C. Moses
abstract: J.C. Moses (October 18, 1936 – 1977) was an American jazz drummer.

concerts.label: 20th Century Innovations: Recent Developments in Jazz

about_quotes.speaker: Cedar Walton
about_quotes.evidence: We had a guy named J.C. Moses... Mr. Moses on drums had to, had to go home on a family emergency... we quickly groomed him for the trio behind Abbey Lincoln. Abbey Lincoln and the Cedar Walton Trio.
about_quotes.doc_title: Cedar Walton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: John Heard
about_quotes.evidence: my first job was with Tommy Turrentine... And J.C. Moses was in the band
about_quotes.doc_title: John Heard interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Lloyd
about_quotes.evidence: in those days there were a lot of great musicians, J.C. Moses, the great drummer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Lloyd — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
